aiashley — global digest Generated 08:18 UTC

Sunday, May 24, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

Sunday saw major developments across multiple fronts. In Washington, a gunman was killed by Secret Service agents after opening fire near the White House; President Trump was unharmed but the incident marks the third shooting near him in a month [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. The US-Iran diplomatic track appears close to resolution, with Secretary of State Rubio indicating an announcement could come within hours, though Tehran disputes reports it has agreed to surrender enriched uranium stockpiles [France 24, Le Monde]. In Ukraine, Russia launched its largest strike on Kyiv in months, killing at least four and injuring over 80, with President Zelensky confirming the use of an Oreshnik hypersonic missile; the attack followed Putin's vow of retaliation for a strike on a dormitory in occupied Luhansk [BBC, France 24]. China's deadliest mining disaster since 2009 claimed at least 82 lives in Shanxi province after a gas explosion [BBC]. Meanwhile, the DRC's Ebola outbreak is accelerating rapidly, with suspected cases nearly tripling in a week to 750, prompting the WHO to raise its risk assessment and the Trump administration to pause deportations to the country [Guardian, Al Jazeera, WHO].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

Gunman Killed by Secret Service After Opening Fire Near White House

  • A 21-year-old man identified as Nasire Best opened fire at a White House checkpoint at approximately 6pm local time on Saturday before being shot and killed by Secret Service agents
  • One bystander was wounded; no Secret Service officers were injured; President Trump was inside the White House but was not affected
  • The suspect was known to the Secret Service and had documented mental health issues; Trump stated the gunman had a 'violent history'
  • This marks the third shooting incident near President Trump in the past month, following incidents at the White House Correspondents' Dinner and near the Washington Monument
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized the suspect's mental health history and prior incidents France 24Focused on the timing amid Iran negotiations Globe and MailHighlighted this as third shooting near Trump in a month Le MondeContent unavailable due to technical issues
02 Conflict

Russia Launches Massive Missile and Drone Attack on Kyiv, Confirms Use of Oreshnik Hypersonic Missile

  • Russia struck Kyiv with approximately 600 drones and 90 missiles overnight Sunday, killing at least four people and injuring more than 80
  • President Zelensky confirmed Russia used the nuclear-capable Oreshnik intermediate-range hypersonic ballistic missile in the strikes
  • Mayor Klitschko reported damage across all districts of Kyiv including residential buildings, schools, and historic Independence Square
  • The attack followed Putin's vow to retaliate after accusing Ukraine of striking a student dormitory in Starobilsk, Luhansk, killing 21 people on Friday
Coverage
BBC WorldProvided detailed context on the Starobilsk strike dispute and Russian accusations France 24Led with casualty figures and Oreshnik confirmation Le MondeContent unavailable due to technical issues
03 Society

At Least 82 Dead in China's Worst Coal Mine Disaster Since 2009

  • A gas explosion at the Liushenyu Coal Mine in Shanxi province killed at least 82 workers, with 2 still missing
  • 247 workers were on duty when the blast occurred at 7:29pm local time Friday; over 100 were rescued
  • 128 people remain hospitalized, most suffering from inhaling poisonous gas, with two in critical condition
  • President Xi Jinping ordered maximum rescue efforts; the state council announced a 'rigorous' investigation with those responsible to be 'severely punished'
Coverage
BBC WorldNoted confusion over initial death toll figures and deployed mine inspection robots
04 Geopolitics

US-Iran Deal Reported Near Completion as Rubio Signals Imminent Announcement

  • Secretary of State Rubio stated an announcement on Iran was possible 'in the next few hours' while speaking in New Delhi
  • A potential deal reportedly includes restoration of Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic to pre-war levels within 30 days
  • US media reported the deal would include Iran handing over highly enriched uranium stockpiles, but an Iranian source denied any such agreement
  • Trump cited the Iran negotiations as reason for remaining in Washington and missing his son's wedding in the Bahamas
Coverage
France 24Detailed both US and Iranian positions, noted Iranian denial of uranium handover Le MondeContent unavailable but headline referenced deal as 'largely negotiated' ABC AustraliaFocused on Trump missing his son's wedding due to negotiations
05 Society

Ebola Outbreak Accelerates in DRC as WHO Raises Risk Assessment

  • Suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo have nearly tripled in one week to approximately 750, with 177 suspected deaths
  • The WHO raised its risk assessment for DRC to 'very high' and warned the outbreak is spreading rapidly across multiple areas
  • Health facilities have come under attack from residents angry about Ebola burial protocols, with a treatment center in Rwampara stormed and tents set on fire in Mongbwalu
  • The Trump administration announced a temporary pause on deportations to DRC due to the outbreak, though one deportee remains stranded in Kinshasa
Coverage
The GuardianEmphasized healthcare system strain and deportation policy implications Al JazeeraFocused on attacks against health workers and community mistrust WHO via GuardianOfficial risk assessment upgrade

  • Romanian director Cristian Mungiu wins second Cannes Palme d'Or for polarization drama 'Fjord' RFI English, Globe and Mail
  • California declares emergency as crews race to prevent explosion of overheating chemical tank in Orange County BBC World, RNZ
  • China to launch astronaut on year-long space station mission as it accelerates moon landing timeline Globe and Mail
  • France bans Israeli minister Ben-Gvir over treatment of detained Gaza flotilla activists RFI English
  • Turkey hosts joint military exercises with both Libyan factions in bid to unify country's forces RFI English
  • Turkish appeals court nullifies opposition CHP leadership election in ruling seen as boosting Erdoğan BBC Europe
  • Over 1.5 million foreign pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia for hajj despite Middle East conflict disruptions France 24
  • Bomb blast near railway track in Quetta, Pakistan injures more than 30 passengers Nikkei Asia
  • India reels under severe heat wave with temperatures exceeding 46°C across multiple states Globe and Mail
